name: Android MCP
what_it_does: MCP server that enables AI agents to interact with Android devices through ADB and Accessibility APIs, supporting gestures, typing, screen capture, and shell commands without requiring computer vision or OCR.
use_cases: Automated QA testing of Android apps via AI agent
UI navigation and interaction on Android devices through natural language
Screen capture and device state inspection for debugging
Automated form filling and app workflows on Android
Accessibility-based UI element discovery without vision models
not_for: Replacing dedicated mobile testing frameworks like Appium for CI/CD pipelines
best_when: You want an AI agent to interact with Android devices for testing, automation, or app navigation without needing computer vision models.
avoid_when: You need iOS support, enterprise-scale device farms, or CI/CD integrated mobile testing. Use Appium or dedicated frameworks instead.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Relies on ADB device authorization (USB debugging must be enabled on the Android device). The connected device implicitly trusts the ADB host.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Fully free and open source. Requires physical Android device or emulator.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No documentation on error handling or error message formats.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: UI interactions are inherently stateful and non-idempotent. Clicking the same button twice does different things depending on app state.
known_agent_gotchas: SECURITY WARNING: Shell-Tool can execute arbitrary commands on the Android device - extremely dangerous with untrusted prompts
README explicitly warns to use only in controlled environments (emulators, test devices)
2-4 second latency per action means complex workflows are slow
UI state is non-deterministic - same action can produce different results depending on app state
SCREENSHOT_QUANTIZED env var affects token usage but tradeoffs not well documented
No built-in safeguards against destructive device operations (factory reset, data deletion)

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
